jokes.text: where the women are robust, the men are pink-cheeked... and the children are pink-cheeked and robust
jokes.setup: Setting up a quaint Prairie Home Companion-style description of a small town
jokes.punchline: The circular, meaningless description where children somehow combine both adult traits
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of Garrison Keillor's verbose, circular descriptions. The redundancy is the joke.

jokes.text: What the hell's so funny? [continues boring story]
jokes.setup: Audience laughing at the purposely boring Prairie Home Companion parody
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated confusion at why people find the deliberately unfunny show amusing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - missing the meta-joke and getting frustrated. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Stupid TV. Be more funny! [Grunts]
jokes.setup: Homer watching unfunny public television
jokes.punchline: His direct, caveman-like command to the TV combined with physical aggression
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Homer - simple, direct, physical solution to abstract problem. High character integration.

jokes.text: You might remember me from such telethons as 'Out with Gout '88' and 'Let's Save Tony Orlando's House'
jokes.setup: Troy's signature 'you might remember me from' line
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake telethon names, especially the oddly specific Tony Orlando reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure moment. Absurd specificity of fake credits is perfectly crafted. High quotability.

jokes.text: Some idiot actually called in
jokes.setup: Pledge drive asking for donations
jokes.punchline: Troy's honest contempt for his audience, said on-air
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of public TV's desperate financial state and performers' true feelings.

jokes.text: Where are the Geraldos? Where are the Eubankses-ses?
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about public TV quality
jokes.punchline: His mangled pluralization of 'Eubanks' shows his education level while demanding lowbrow TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer linguistic fumble. Character-specific grammar mistake while demanding trash TV.

jokes.text: Marjorie, please. I enjoy all the meats of our cultural stew.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to sound cultured about ballet
jokes.punchline: Overwrought metaphor using 'meats' - very Homer way to describe culture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer line. Tries to sound sophisticated but uses food metaphor and awkward phrasing. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Ahh, ballet. Hee-hee-hee. All right. Whoo-hoo! Yea!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to love ballet
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated, inappropriate celebration like he's at a sports game
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer physical comedy. Shows his misunderstanding of ballet etiquette.

jokes.text: That's one right there. All right. But, remember, it's mine.
jokes.setup: Ruth asks to borrow a power sander, Homer says he doesn't have one
jokes.punchline: Power sander is literally running right there, but Homer's possessiveness overrides helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's selfishness and contradiction within seconds.

jokes.text: That's what ballet is? [Groans]
jokes.setup: Homer's excitement about going to ballet
jokes.punchline: His dismay upon seeing actual ballet dancers (visual), realizing his misconception
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to Homer's earlier enthusiasm. His disappointed realization is relatable.

jokes.text: You can't back out like when you volunteered for that army experiment to avoid dinner at my sisters'
jokes.setup: Marge trying to hold Homer to his ballet promise
jokes.punchline: Reveals Homer volunteered for dangerous military experiments just to avoid family dinner
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Homer's extreme avoidance tactics. Sets up the flashback perfectly.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, you do realize this may result in hair loss, giddiness and the loss of equilibrium? Yeah, yeah, yeah. Just give me the serum.
jokes.setup: Doctor warning Homer about experimental serum side effects
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive acceptance of dangerous side effects just to avoid dinner
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Homer's impatience and poor priorities on full display.

jokes.text: It was worth it. [Body thuds on floor] [Homer giggling]
jokes.setup: Homer claiming the army experiment was worth avoiding dinner
jokes.punchline: Immediately collapses from the serum's effects while giggling maniacally
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and irony. Homer's claim immediately undercut by physical collapse and drugged laughter.

jokes.text: Gonna go see the bear in the little car, huh?
jokes.setup: Homer mentions taking his wife to ballet
jokes.punchline: Barney's confused association of ballet with circus acts
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Barney's addled understanding of culture. Absurd but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Mmm, invisible cola.
jokes.setup: Homer at vending machine
jokes.punchline: Homer drinking air after apparently getting nothing from the machine but finding it satisfying
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Homer's ability to find satisfaction in disappointment.

jokes.text: Time to stick it to the man.
jokes.setup: Homer about to use vending machine
jokes.punchline: Treating a simple purchase as revolutionary act against authority
jokes.explanation: Mild irony about Homer's small rebellion. Sets up the vending machine mishap.

jokes.text: That's just an old wives' tale.
jokes.setup: Warned that someone lost an arm in the vending machine
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissal immediately before getting trapped himself
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate comeuppance. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: I'm stuck. Help me. He's done for! Let's get outta here!
jokes.setup: Homer gets stuck in vending machine
jokes.punchline: His friends immediately abandon him rather than help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Lenny and Carl's cowardice. Physical comedy payoff works.

jokes.text: Must... get... to ballet. Promised Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer stuck in vending machine
jokes.punchline: His determination to keep his promise despite ridiculous predicament
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Homer's commitment to Marge despite his situation.

jokes.text: Snack-related mishap.
jokes.setup: Homer calling for help while stuck in vending machine
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic euphemism for his ridiculous predicament
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement. Perfect economy and quotability. Could only work in The Simpsons universe.

jokes.text: Sure, Homer. Trapped in vending machines. Okay.
jokes.setup: Homer calling to explain why he'll miss ballet
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic disbelief at Homer's truthful but absurd excuse
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamic - Marge's reasonable skepticism of Homer's incredible but true story.

jokes.text: When my husband left, he took all our power tools... along with the car, my youth, my faith in mankind.
jokes.setup: Ruth explaining why she needs to borrow tools
jokes.punchline: Escalates from practical items to existential losses in deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane to profound. Dark humor delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Vyachepov's loins oughta be outlawed.
jokes.setup: Women at ballet making comments about dancers
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sexual comment about male ballet dancer
jokes.explanation: Mild shock humor about middle-aged women objectifying dancers. Somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Far too much dancing, not nearly enough prancing. A little mincing would be nice.
jokes.setup: Critiquing the ballet performance
jokes.punchline: Overly specific, affected vocabulary to sound sophisticated about dance
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing pretentious ballet criticism. Captures character voice well.

jokes.text: It's almost 9:30. [Laughs] Yeah, right. We better turn in. [Laughs, stops] Oh, you were serious.
jokes.setup: Marge saying it's getting late at 9:30
jokes.punchline: Ruth's realization that Marge actually considers 9:30 PM late
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - suburban mom vs. single woman lifestyle differences.

jokes.text: I'm going to have these things on my arms forever. [Wedding March plays]
jokes.setup: Homer with vending machine arms attached
jokes.punchline: Wedding march plays as if he's marrying the vending machines
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag treating the attachment as a marriage ceremony. Good absurd logic.

jokes.text: They'll grow back, right? Oh, yeah. Whew.
jokes.setup: Doctor about to saw off Homer's arms
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive question and relief at obvious lie about arms regrowing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's gullibility and the doctor's casual deception.

jokes.text: Homer, are you just holding on to the can? Your point being?
jokes.setup: Doctor realizes Homer might not actually be stuck
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response suggests he could let go but doesn't want to
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that Homer's predicament is partly self-imposed. Perfect character logic and timing.

jokes.text: All he ever did was eat, sleep and drink beer. Your point being?
jokes.setup: Ruth complaining about her ex-husband's habits
jokes.punchline: Marge's response mirrors Homer's earlier line, revealing she sees nothing wrong with those habits
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Marge's acceptance of Homer's lifestyle. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Hey, can I throw up in your bathroom? I'll buy something.
jokes.setup: Ruth feeling sick
jokes.punchline: Treating vomiting as a commercial transaction requiring purchase
jokes.explanation: Absurd politeness about bodily functions. Good awkward humor with transactional element.

jokes.text: Mrs. Simpson: While we were rescuing your husband, a lumberyard burned down.
jokes.setup: Homer having firemen write a note to verify his vending machine story
jokes.punchline: The note reveals his rescue caused a much more serious disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation. Homer's minor problem caused major disaster. Perfect dark comedy structure.

jokes.text: D'oh! Lumber has a million uses.
jokes.setup: Learning about the lumberyard fire
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced concern about lumber's versatility rather than the disaster he caused
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer misplaced priorities. Focuses on lumber utility instead of accepting responsibility.

jokes.text: Marge, you can't go out on Saturday. That's our special night. What's so special about it? A little show called Dr. Quinn, Medicine Woman?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming Saturday is their special night
jokes.punchline: The 'special' activity is watching a cheesy TV medical drama
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's low standards for romance. Period reference dates it.

jokes.text: Where're you going? I don't know. When will you be home? I'm not sure. Where're you going? You already asked me that.
jokes.setup: Homer interrogating Marge about her night out
jokes.punchline: His anxious repetition and Marge's pointed observation of his neediness
jokes.explanation: Realistic marital dynamic. Homer's clingy behavior and Marge's exasperation ring true.

jokes.text: How can you do this, Marge? How can you desert your children? Have a blast, Mom. Rock the Casbah.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically claiming Marge is deserting the family
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately contradict him by encouraging their mom
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Homer's guilt trip. Kids see through his manipulation.

jokes.text: Sunshine, lollipops and rainbows ev-- Oh. Sorry, Marge. Wrong tape.
jokes.setup: Ruth starting to play music for their night out
jokes.punchline: Accidentally plays wholesome children's music instead of rebellious music
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between intended rebellious mood and accidental wholesomeness.

jokes.text: Don't worry. You'll feel better once we put your hair up in curlers and give you a makeover, Homina. [High-pitched voice] Oh, that would be delight-- [Normal voice] Quiet, boy.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting a makeover to cheer up Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer briefly embracing feminine activities before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's brief feminine moment before masculine reassertion. Mild gender comedy.

jokes.text: Sure, Dad. [Imitates whip cracking]
jokes.setup: Lisa supposedly agreeing that women have a right to go out
jokes.punchline: Makes whip-cracking sound implying Homer is whipped/controlled
jokes.explanation: Standard 'whipped husband' joke. Lisa's precocious understanding of marital dynamics.

jokes.text: Well, howdilly dodilly-do. [Line clicks, dial tone] Hello? Hello? Hellodilly-odilly?
jokes.setup: Homer calling Ned for companionship
jokes.punchline: Ned's enthusiastic greeting cut short by Homer hanging up, then continuing to talk to dial tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders speech pattern. His oblivious continuation after hang-up is very in-character.

jokes.text: Haven't you seen Home Alone? If some burglars come, it'll be a very humorous and entertaining situation.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying they need a babysitter
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of movie vs. reality regarding child endangerment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's poor parenting logic and pop culture confusion.

jokes.text: Of course, being a highly skilled attorney... my fee is $175 an hour. We pay eight dollars for the night... and you can take two Popsicles out of the freezer. Three. Two. Okay, two, and I get to keep this old birdcage. Done. Still got it.
jokes.setup: Lionel Hutz offering expensive legal services as babysitting
jokes.punchline: Immediately negotiates down to almost nothing plus random items, then celebrates the 'victory'
jokes.explanation: Classic Lionel Hutz - terrible negotiation skills while thinking he's winning. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: How come no one else's chair is doing this?
jokes.setup: Homer at country bar in mechanical bull or similar contraption
jokes.punchline: His confusion about why his seat is bucking while others sit normally
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to mechanical bull concept. Standard fish-out-of-water humor.

jokes.text: I am lucky. I have a husband and three wonderful children. Thank you very much.
jokes.setup: Creepy guy hitting on Marge asking if she wants to get lucky
jokes.punchline: Marge's sincere, wholesome interpretation of 'getting lucky'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response. Her genuine wholesomeness deflects crude pickup line beautifully.

jokes.text: Sometimes you gotta go where everybody knows your name.
jokes.setup: Leaving the hostile bar
jokes.punchline: Cheers theme song reference while going to completely opposite environment
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference. Works as transition but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We're phasing out the games. People drink less when they're having fun.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to play bar games
jokes.punchline: Moe's cynical business logic about keeping customers miserable to increase alcohol sales
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about bar psychology. Perfect Moe line - cynical business wisdom delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Like lawyers work in big skyscrapers and have secretaries. Look at him. He's wearing a belt. That's Hollywood for you.
jokes.setup: Hutz watching TV show about lawyers
jokes.punchline: His complaints about unrealistic lawyer portrayals while being completely incompetent himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hutz delusion. The belt detail is absurdly specific and reveals his low standards.

jokes.text: Oh, wow. I've wasted my life.
jokes.setup: Marge trying a 'smart drink' at underground club
jokes.punchline: Immediate existential realization about her boring suburban life
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment. Sudden clarity about her sheltered existence is relatable.

jokes.text: Mayor Quimby! What are you doing here? I'm here with my nephews.
jokes.setup: Marge encounters Mayor at underground club
jokes.punchline: Quimby's obvious lie about being with 'nephews' at inappropriate venue
jokes.explanation: Standard politician hypocrisy joke. Quimby's transparent lie is appropriately sleazy.

jokes.text: It's Garrett Morris's birthday.
jokes.setup: Underground club scene
jokes.punchline: Random celebration of obscure SNL cast member's birthday
jokes.explanation: Obscure reference that was probably funnier in 1993. Random celebrity birthday as party theme.

jokes.text: This is not a library. This is not a Kwik-E-Mart.
jokes.setup: Apu at underground club
jokes.punchline: His confused attempts to orient himself in unfamiliar environment
jokes.explanation: Apu fish-out-of-water moment. His methodical elimination of familiar places is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You're not gonna hunt me for sport are you?
jokes.setup: Ruth showing Marge something outdoors
jokes.punchline: Marge's paranoid leap to most extreme possibility when shown gun
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge paranoia. Her mind goes to 'Most Dangerous Game' scenario immediately. Great dark comedy.

jokes.text: I hit it! I hit it! My cans. My precious, antique cans. Aw, look what you done to 'em.
jokes.setup: Marge successfully shooting at target practice
jokes.punchline: Owner's dismay at his 'precious antique cans' being shot (the whole point of target practice)
jokes.explanation: Good irony about shooting range targets being 'precious.' Marge's excitement contrasts with owner's dismay.

jokes.text: Homer, stop that. It's just a weather station. Come on, Marge. It's fun to smash things. I smashed it good.
jokes.setup: At the make-out point, Homer wants to vandalize
jokes.punchline: Marge's reasonable objection vs. Homer's childish destructive joy
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer/Marge dynamic. His destructive impulses vs. her responsibility.

jokes.text: As of this moment, Lionel Hutz no longer exists. Say hello to Miguel Sanchez.
jokes.setup: Hutz burning his papers and documents
jokes.punchline: His dramatic identity change with stereotypical Hispanic name
jokes.explanation: Classic Hutz incompetence. Announces new identity to witnesses. The name choice is absurdly generic.

jokes.text: Hey, a new weather station! I'll bash it good. Oh... it's just no fun without Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer alone at the overlook, sees another weather station
jokes.punchline: His vandalistic impulse deflated by loneliness for his wife
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback showing Homer misses sharing experiences with Marge, even destructive ones.

jokes.text: Ah, just get one of those inflatable women. But make sure it's a woman though. 'Cause one time I-- [Stammering]
jokes.setup: Cletus giving Homer advice about loneliness
jokes.punchline: His advice trails into uncomfortable personal revelation he catches himself sharing
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor about Cletus's implied sexual mishap. The cutoff preserves some dignity.

jokes.text: Their left taillight's a little smaller than their right one. I better pull 'em over.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum observing Ruth's car
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous reasoning for initiating what becomes a major chase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic. Absurd attention to meaningless detail while missing actual crimes.

jokes.text: This car's stolen. Fat chance.
jokes.setup: Police trying to pull them over
jokes.punchline: Ruth's casual revelation that they're in a stolen car, Marge's sarcastic disbelief
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation moment. Ruth's casual confession and Marge's 'fat chance' irony work perfectly.

jokes.text: One is wearing a green dress, pearls and has a lot of blue hair. A lot of blue hair? [Giggles] What a freak.
jokes.setup: Police description of Marge being broadcast
jokes.punchline: Lou's reaction to Marge's distinctive hair as freakish, despite knowing her
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lou's unprofessional commentary and Marge's distinctive appearance.

jokes.text: It's Marge! She's become a crazed criminal just because I didn't take her to the ballet. That's exactly how Dillinger got started. Really?
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Marge on TV chase
jokes.punchline: His guilt about causing her 'criminal career' and Barney's gullible response to fake Dillinger origin story
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character work. Homer's guilt manifesting as criminal psychology theory, Barney's credulity.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It just disappeared. It's a ghost car! There are ghost cars all over these highways, you know. Hold me. Only if you hold me.
jokes.setup: Police lose sight of Ruth's car
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's supernatural explanation and their mutual fear/comfort seeking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - supernatural explanation for basic police incompetence. The mutual holding is sweet.

jokes.text: This cross-country flight from the law would be hell if we didn't stick together. Hey. Friends stick together. It's amazing how through all this adversity, we managed to stick together. If there's one thing decent folk do, it's stick together. I hate it when the waffles stick together. Sticking together is what good waffles do.
jokes.setup: Conversation about friendship and loyalty
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition of 'stick together' until it becomes absurd waffle discussion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation gag. The phrase becomes meaningless through repetition, ending in waffle absurdity.

jokes.text: Mmm, engine-block eggs. If we can keep these down, we'll be sitting pretty. That's them! Quiet. I can't hear the eggs.
jokes.setup: Ruth cooking eggs on car engine, police approaching
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's bizarre priority of listening to eggs over police work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum distraction. Engine-block cooking is creative, his egg-listening priority is absurd.

jokes.text: Don't touch my stuff! Hey. This isn't the Y.M.C.A.
jokes.setup: Hutz has been babysitting overnight
jokes.punchline: His territorial behavior and confused location awareness
jokes.explanation: Hutz's disorientation and homeless implications. His confusion about where he is fits character.

jokes.text: I'm on a road. Looks to be asphalt. Um, aw, geez. Trees, shrubs. I'm directly under the Earth's sun... now.
jokes.setup: Dispatch asking Wiggum for his location
jokes.punchline: His overly literal, unhelpful description ending with cosmic perspective
jokes.explanation: Legendary Wiggum moment. Perfect escalation from obvious to philosophical. 'Earth's sun' is brilliant absurdity.

jokes.text: I always knew someday Mom would violently rise up and cast off the shackles of our male oppressors. Aw, shut your yap.
jokes.setup: Lisa reacting to news of Marge's criminal behavior
jokes.punchline: Her feminist interpretation immediately undercut by Bart's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voices. Lisa's intellectual analysis vs. Bart's crude dismissal. Good sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: At the risk of editorializing, these women are guilty and must be dealt with in a harsh and brutal fashion. Otherwise, their behavior could incite other women, leading to anarchy of biblical proportions. It's in Revelations, people!
jokes.setup: TV news coverage of the chase
jokes.punchline: Anchor abandoning journalistic objectivity for extreme misogynistic panic
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire. Escalation from 'at risk of editorializing' to biblical apocalypse is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Oh, no! They're headed right for the Grand Chasm. Oh, my God! They're gonna drive right into it just to teach us men a lesson. And it's all my fault!
jokes.setup: Car chase approaching cliff
jokes.punchline: Homer's guilt manifesting as belief that Marge would commit suicide to make a point
jokes.explanation: Good character psychology. Homer's guilt makes him think everything is about him, even potential suicide.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I haven't been a better husband. I'm sorry about the time I tried to make gravy in the bathtub. I'm sorry I used your wedding dress to wax the car. I'm sorry-- Oh, well, let's just say I'm sorry for the whole marriage up to this point.
jokes.setup: Homer's desperate apology to potentially dying Marge
jokes.punchline: Escalation through specific bizarre offenses to wholesale marriage condemnation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure. Specific absurd crimes building to total marriage condemnation is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: You're right. I am lucky to have him.
jokes.setup: Homer's comprehensive apology for being a terrible husband
jokes.punchline: Marge's sincere response finding the positive in his admission of total failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response. Her ability to find love in Homer's comprehensive confession of failure is touching and funny.

jokes.text: Ha! And to think those idiot environmentalists were protesting this landfill. Solid waste. I could kiss you.
jokes.setup: Car crash survivors land safely in garbage dump
jokes.punchline: Quimby's vindication of controversial landfill because it saved lives
jokes.explanation: Good political irony. Environmental controversy resolved by accidental life-saving function.

jokes.text: Lionel Hutz, a.k.a. Miguel Sanchez, a.k.a. Dr. Nguyen Van Thoc... was paid eight dollars for his 32 hours of babysitting. He was glad to get it.
jokes.setup: Epilogue about characters' fates
jokes.punchline: Hutz's identity crisis expanded with Asian name, plus his terrible hourly rate
jokes.explanation: Good callback expanding Hutz's identity confusion. The wage calculation and his satisfaction with it is perfect.
